tax: Rime Creeper is placed at species rank beneath Rimeling (Glacial order, Water Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a creeping rime-ice body, freezing-advance that locks prey, and melt-failure in warmth. It is separated from its sibling the Hail Sleetling by its slow creeping ground-frost versus the Sleetling's flung ice-shot; the two share the genus body-plan but never the flung projectiles. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: rime and needle-ice grown from frozen seep-water.
ap: Rime Creeper stands indefinite creeping front, up to 3 m across. Its silhouette is an advancing crust of feathery rime with a freezing edge. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a leading edge of fresh needle-ice growing visibly outward; a cold that frosts breath and fittings before the ice is touched; and a crackling tick as it freezes new water into its body. It moves with a slow creep across any wet or damp surface, freezing as it goes, halted by warmth, and shows a pale ice-sheen, no native light. Even after dispersal it can be identified from a sheet of rime that later melts to a wet stain ringed with frost-cracks, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Rime Creeper persists in cold seeps, ice-rimed galleries, and chill high-tier passages where freezing water can be grown. It does not breed; it forms where water-pressure animates a cold seep and freezes it into a creeping rime body, and recurs whenever the cold seep refreezes after a thaw and the rime re-advances. It sustains its mass on cold-seep water frozen into the body, drawing in ambient water and damp, frozen and added to its mass. Range is bounded by the cold zone of its seep: outside that band the body melts and fails in any warmth. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a damp patch that grows feathery rime against a warm draught, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Rime Creeper is roused by contact with its freezing edge or warmth that it can chill and otherwise lies a still rime-sheet posing as natural frost. It claims a single cold gallery and answers intrusion with freezing over feet and gear to lock prey in place. Toward grey-folk it is a slow trap that immobilises; survey crews report that Mist-Touched scouts watch for breath-frost in a still gallery as the first sign. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Rime Creeper: a body of rime and needle-ice grown from frozen seep-water; immune to cold and to piercing, which only chips its crust; and a defining weakness — fire and warmth, which melt it at once. Its senses run through the medium itself: cold and moisture-gradient sensing. It tolerates cold, depth, and darkness but fails at warmth and fire. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the creeping rime-front and freezing edge, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Rime Creeper are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it freezes water and damp to grow its body; it locks feet and gear in fresh ice to immobilise; it advances a freezing front across any wet surface; and it refreezes from a buried ice-seed after seeming to melt. Each is a direct expression of the freezing of seep-water into rime, so it cannot, for instance, produce heat, fling projectiles, or survive warmth. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Rime Creeper matters because it locks crews in place in cold galleries, and breath-frost in still air is now the first mask-and-move signal. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as an immobilising cold hazard logged with cold seeps, and its presence reshapes routes through the high-tier cold galleries, now crossed quickly before rime can lock footing. Feathery rime growing against a warm draught marks an advancing Rime Creeper.
g: Rime Creeper (Glacies repens) is a glacial elemental of creeping rime-ice that advances as a freezing crust and locks prey in place. Documented from the cold seeps and ice-rimed galleries of the high tiers, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a spreading crust of needle-ice and rime fed by cold seep — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. Unlike liquid kin it spreads by freezing the water around it, growing its body from whatever it chills. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a patch of melting rime and a wet stain where the ice gave way and no carcass.
